mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL indicates accelerating inflation, which forces discount rates higher and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for CPIAUCSL slope exceeds 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive 20d returns occur in >65% of rising inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in UNRATE exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >55% of threshold-crossing events, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in GDPC1 and HOUST reflects broad-based consumer and housing demand growth, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining INDPRO during a monetary tightening cycle indicates supply-side contraction that outweighs rate hike benefits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in >58% of instances, or regime-split IC shows no directional divergence.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS indicates stronger consumer credit availability and spending capacity, which immediately boosts equity risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS slope shows negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of rising credit periods.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital becomes prohibitive for marginal equities, suppressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in INDPRO during a post-shock recovery phase signal persistent supply chain bottlenecks that delay earnings rebounds, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops in recovery regimes yield positive 5-day returns in >65% of cases, or regime-specific IC is not significantly different from zero.
